tasks.name: implement-force-stop-repository
tasks.task:
Read plans/pipeline-force-stop.md for the full design before writing any code. Foundation and infrastructure layers must already be committed.

Implement the repository layer changes for the pipeline-force-stop feature:

1. Modify src/repositories/ports/agent.ts (File 9 in the plan)
   - Add optional third parameter `signal?: AbortSignal` to the dispatch() method on IClaudeCodeRepository
   - This is a non-breaking change (optional param)

2. Modify src/repositories/agentRepository.ts (File 10 in the plan)
   - Update the dispatch() implementation signature to accept signal?
   - Forward signal as the last argument when calling this.infra.runClaude(..., signal)

Run ts_checker after all changes.

tasks.name: review-force-stop-repository
tasks.task:
target_path: src/repositories
ng_output_path: .claude/tmp/review-force-stop-repository

Review the pipeline-force-stop repository layer changes:
1. src/repositories/ports/agent.ts: IClaudeCodeRepository.dispatch() has new optional third param signal?: AbortSignal
2. src/repositories/agentRepository.ts: dispatch() implementation accepts and forwards signal to this.infra.runClaude()
3. No business logic added to repository layer
4. No layer violations: repositories/ must not import from domains/ or services/

tasks.command: npm run lint:fix && npm run build && npm run test:unit

tasks.name: commit-force-stop-repository
tasks.task:
Repository layer changes are complete and passing. Commit the following files with an appropriate conventional commit message:
- src/repositories/ports/agent.ts (signal? added to dispatch)
- src/repositories/agentRepository.ts (signal forwarded to runClaude)

Suggested message: feat(repository): forward AbortSignal through dispatch to runClaude
